title: 'Protect children's future': Centre seeks dialogue with oppn amid protests - top developments
content: Parliament witnessed disruptions over the alleged NEET paper leak and police action. Opposition MPs marked a 'Black Day' protest demanding the education minister's resignation. The government offered a parliamentary discussion, which the opposition refused without the minister stepping down. Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike, setting conditions for ending it. Courts are examining allegations of police brutality during student demonstrations.

title: 'Brokerage fee': Pakistan seeks $10bn lifeline, after mediation role between US & Iran
content: Pakistan seeks a ten billion dollar financial lifeline from the Trump administration. This facility aims to strengthen its foreign exchange reserves and ease rupee pressure. The request follows Pakistan's diplomatic role in facilitating US-Iran contacts. Economic stakes are considerable as Pakistan narrowly avoided default in 2023. The US is considering the proposal, which involves strategic and financial calculations.

title: Centre unlikely to sack Pradhan over NEET row, open to compensate victims' kin: Report
content: The central government will likely not remove the education minister amid NEET paper leak demands.  It believes he is not responsible for the alleged examination irregularities.  The government may consider compensation for students' families who died by suicide.  It is also open to talks with protesters led by the CJP.  Parliament remains the appropriate forum for discussing the issue and finding solutions.

title: 'I sent a message to Alexis': Father reveals truth behind Messi's viral pep talks
content: Lionel Messi's viral speech before the World Cup final was tactical.  His teammate's father clarified the captain's words were taken out of context.  Messi urged players to focus on football and their game plan.  This explanation dismisses speculation of internal team issues.  Spain ultimately won the match, proving to be the better side.

title: Terrorist kill J&K cop in Anantnag market, first strike since Pahalgam attack
content: A J&K police head constable was killed by a terrorist in Anantnag's busy market.  The attack occurred during the ongoing Amarnath Yatra, which has faced disruptions.  Security forces have launched a search and detained several individuals for questioning.  This incident marks the first attack in Kashmir since a previous massacre.  Officials condemned the act, vowing that it would not go unpunished.

title: Ford & Aramark probing Ford factory kiosks after employees lost jobs for 'stealing'
content: Ford and Aramark are reviewing workplace self-service kiosks. This review follows employee dismissals for alleged snack theft incidents. Workers claim payment failures and software glitches caused wrongful accusations. Some recently fired employees have been reinstated after investigations cleared them. The companies are investigating kiosk functionality issues in limited cases.

title: Tropical storm Bertha makes landfall: Strong winds and flooding threat looms in US
content: Tropical Storm Bertha made landfall in southern Louisiana with strong winds.  The storm brought storm surge and heavy rain to the US Gulf Coast.  Forecasters expect the system to weaken steadily after moving inland.  Meanwhile, severe storms caused tornado reports and flash floods elsewhere.  Hurricane Fausto remains offshore in the eastern Pacific.

title: Scientists heard bird sounds beneath floating Solar Farm in Netherlands
content: Floating solar farms in the Netherlands may be supporting local wildlife. Researchers observed bird sounds suggesting possible nesting under the solar panels. Underwater shelters installed beneath the panels attracted hundreds of fish and thousands of aquatic creatures. These structures developed into small ecosystems supporting life higher up the food chain. The findings suggest carefully designed farms could create new habitats for wildlife.

title: Michael Jordan’s moving words on coping with his father’s tragic murder
content: The NBA legend Michael Jackson achieved a lot of wealth and accolades but his personal life was devastated by the los of his father as he was brutally murdered. The NBA icon has opened up how it destroyed his family.

title: Egypt reopens 3,400-year-old tomb of Amenhotep III after 20 years
content: The ancient tomb of Amenhotep III has reopened after a 20-year hiatus, inviting travelers to delve into Egypt's rich history. Set to coincide with the Grand Egyptian Museum's forthcoming launch, the tomb's restoration has revitalized its stunning wall art and hieroglyphs. Once a resting place for the pharaoh and his queens, this site showcases significant ancient beliefs while aiming to attract tourists eager to explore its historical treasures.

title: Giant Antarctic glacier is melting from below. Can an 80km curtain stop it?
content: In an exciting scientific endeavor, researchers are exploring the idea of constructing a massive underwater curtain to defend Thwaites Glacier against warm ocean currents. This strategic barrier is intended to redirect these warmer waters, aiming to curb the glacier's swift melting. A recent expedition gathered vital information from the Amundsen Sea to determine the project's feasibility, while additional studies in Svalbard focus on understanding the environmental ramifications of the barrier.

title: NYC Mayor Mamdani hires 'tech crew': Here's what they will actually do
content: New York City has opened applications for a new kind of government job. Mayor Zohran Mamdani is hiring software engineers, product designers, user researchers and data experts for Public Interest Technology Crews—around 30 full-time roles across four teams, backed by $5.24 million in the city budget. Their first build is a complaint portal enforcing New York's Click to Cancel subscription rule, live from October 1.

title: Jaishankar pushes for fair trade, supply chain reliability in talks with Wang Yi
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar met his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi in Manila. They discussed fair market access and trade balance, and supply chain reliability. Border peace and tranquility remain a prerequisite for normal bilateral ties between the two nations. India welcomed recent measures to normalize Sino-Indian relations and direct flights. Both sides engaged to ensure border peace since October 2024.

title: Lalit Modi says his home will remain London but will now visit India after winning tribunal
content: Lalit Modi announced his return to India after SAFEMA tribunal cleared penalties. He will apply for an OCI card as he surrendered his Indian passport. Modi claimed he fled India due to threats from Dawood Ibrahim. He stated he has won all his legal cases in India. Modi will continue residing in his London mansion while visiting India.

title: Candace Owens slams Erika Kirk, says Charlie Kirk fueled her fame ambitions
content: Candace Owens intensified her public feud with Erika Kirk, accusing her of pursuing fame and fortune throughout her life. In a podcast, Owens claimed Charlie Kirk was Erika's "ticket to more fame" and argued a recent article criticizing her instead exposed Erika's character. She also questioned Erika's motives, saying her focus had always been on public attention, money, and influence.

title: Two-time Olympic rowing champion Olaf Tufte dies at 50
content: Olaf Tufte, Norway’s two-time Olympic rowing champion, has died at the age of 50 after being found unconscious at his family farm. His career stretched across seven Olympic Games and included two Olympic gold medals and six World Championship medals. Tributes from leaders, fellow athletes and World Rowing remember him as a champion, mentor and a respected friend.

title: Pat McAfee fires back as fans blame his $65M ESPN deal for layoffs
content: As ESPN makes headlines with significant layoffs, Pat McAfee faces backlash for his notable contract negotiations. Supporters link his financial discussions to the job cuts, but McAfee clarifies that these decisions are part of separate budgeting issues. Yet, the unfavorable optics of his arrangement complicate his defense, prompting a deeper examination of ESPN's commitment to its workforce.

title: Leo Tolstoy’s quote on love: Why letting go of blame helps you grow
content: Leo Tolstoy, a renowned writer and thinker, profoundly influenced global leaders with his ideas. His novels explored human psychology and societal complexities with great depth. Tolstoy advocated for personal change, emphasizing compassion and non-violence over blame. This philosophy continues to resonate, guiding individuals toward inner growth and better relationships. His teachings highlight that societal improvement begins with individual transformation and responsible action.

title: Couple dead, 4 hurt as boulder falls on tempo on Jammu-Srinagar NH in Ramban
content: A massive boulder struck a tempo on the Jammu-Srinagar highway, killing two people and injuring four others.  Meanwhile, officials narrowly escaped separate landslides on the Jammu-Poonch highway.  Vehicular movement on the Jammu-Srinagar highway was suspended due to multiple landslides.  Relentless rainfall battered the Jammu region, triggering further mudslides and shooting stones.  All major pilgrimages have remained suspended in Jammu and Kashmir since July 19.

title: Jantar Mantar boils again: Stones pelted, 6 cops injured, internet suspended
content: Fresh clashes erupted between protesters and security personnel at Jantar Mantar. Police fired tear gas and used batons to disperse the agitated crowd. At least six police personnel, including two ACPs, sustained injuries during the unrest. Authorities activated internet jammers in parts of central Delhi amid heightened security. The situation remained tense as the crowd at Jantar Mantar swelled significantly.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for July 23
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 23, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Ousted Venezuelan president Maduro to face US drug trafficking trial in June 2027
content: Ousted Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro and his wife will face trial in the United States.  They are charged with drug trafficking and could receive life imprisonment if convicted.  Their defense will challenge the indictment and the legality of their capture.  US prosecutors allege Maduro facilitated cocaine shipments into the United States.  The trial is scheduled to begin on June 1, 2027.

title: 'Sooryavanshi must have rectified where he went wrong': Iyer ahead of Zimbabwe series
content: Captain Shreyas Iyer expressed confidence in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ahead of the Zimbabwe series.  The young batter faced challenges during his recent England tour.  Iyer believes Sooryavanshi has learned from his experiences and is ready.  The team aims to play with freedom and recover from past tours.  Sooryavanshi feels supported and confident returning to Harare.

title: Salman Khan backs students' protest, says they deserve credit for peaceful movement'
content: Bollywood star Salman Khan has voiced his support for students protesting against paper leaks, calling the issue "very serious" and urging that the movement remain focused on education rather than politics. Taking to Instagram, Salman praised students for coming together to demand a better education system and also acknowledged the support extended by their parents. However, he expressed sadness over reports that the otherwise peaceful protests had turned violent.

title: Why proposed US-Saudi nuclear deal has triggered alarm over proliferation risks
content: The Trump administration is advancing a civil nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia. This pact could allow uranium enrichment and spent fuel processing by the kingdom. Critics fear this weakens global non-proliferation norms and may trigger an arms race. Saudi Arabia seeks nuclear power for economic diversification and reduced fossil fuel reliance. The deal faces congressional review and potential opposition due to its safeguards.

title: 'Aam'ezing voyage: UP's mangoes make first sea journey to Dubai
content: Indian mangoes reached Dubai via a 25-day sea journey.  Nearly ninety percent of the fruit remained market-ready after transit.  This successful shipment paves the way for cheaper and larger mango exports.  The new protocol reduces reliance on expensive air freight for shipments.  This development enhances farmer incomes and boosts export earnings.

title: From Miami to Maui: Jeff Bezos' $800 million-plus real estate empire
content: Jeff Bezos has amassed a vast residential portfolio across the United States. His property collection spans from Washington state to South Florida and Hawaii. Bezos acquired multiple neighboring properties to expand his existing compounds. He also owns two luxury yachts, Koru and Abeona, which are assets afloat. This extensive real estate buying pattern reflects his significant wealth and investments.

title: 'For the sake of India's future': Yuvraj calls for 'dialogue' amid NEET protests
content: Yuvraj Singh urged dialogue and peace for students amid NEET exam protests. He stressed that children deserve safe learning environments to chase their dreams. Protests over the alleged NEET paper leak have intensified across the country. Parliament has faced disruptions, and legal scrutiny continues over the issue. Manu Bhaker also voiced concerns for students' futures and fair opportunities.

title: Abhijeet Bhattacharya defends Udit Narayan over kissing controversy, questions 'issue'
content: Playback singer Abhijeet Bhattacharya has revealed that he advised Udit Narayan to openly acknowledge the viral kissing controversy instead of shying away from it, saying he personally stood by his fellow singer when the backlash erupted. Speaking in a recent interview, Abhijeet recalled telling Udit to accept the incident instead of being defensive.

title: Trump's 200% tariff plan may be a bitter pill for US consumers, warns Dr Reddy's
content: The United States plans significant tariffs on imported generic medicines. These duties will rise to 200% in the coming years. Dr Reddy's Laboratories anticipates higher medicine prices for American consumers. Shifting manufacturing to the US is currently considered impractical by the company. India faces substantial exposure as a major generic medicine exporter to the US.

title: 'He was struggling': Ex-India cricketer slams Shubman Gill's captaincy vs England
content: Former India batter Hanuma Vihari questioned Shubman Gill's leadership after a recent ODI defeat. Vihari highlighted concerns regarding the team's limited bowling options during the crucial match. He also expressed confusion over Kuldeep Yadav's exclusion from the playing eleven. The former player emphasized the need for experienced match-winners in ODIs. Vihari believes Gill needs tactical improvements in his captaincy approach.

title: Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa to revisit Shilpa Shinde-Vikas Gupta rivalry
content: The upcoming episodes of Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa are set to bring one of television’s most talked-about rivalries back into the spotlight. Vikas Gupta has shot for a special appearance on the reality show, where he comes face-to-face with Shilpa Shinde years after their public fallout. Their bitter feud, which dates back to Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain! and intensified during Bigg Boss 11, has remained one of TV’s most discussed conflicts. Sources reveal that their interactions are expected to be among the biggest highlights of the upcoming episodes, promising drama as the two share screen space once again.

title: From oil to atoms: Donald Trump to open nuclear door to Saudis
content: The Trump White House is set to announce a significant nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia. This agreement allows for extensive civilian nuclear cooperation and uranium enrichment on Saudi soil. Critics express concerns about nuclear proliferation and regional security implications. The US administration argues this approach counters Russian and Chinese influence in the region. Lawmakers will scrutinize the deal, which may weaken existing non-proliferation safeguards.

title: Meghalaya homestay mission: How to get 70% subsidy to start or upgrade a hostel
content: Meghalaya is offering a 70 percent subsidy for homestay development ahead of the National Games 2027. This initiative aims to create entrepreneurship and long-term tourism businesses for local residents. Applicants can receive grants for new constructions and upgrades, with financial and technical support provided. The scheme also includes training programs and digital platform listings for increased visibility.

title: Watch: TMC MLA Kunal Ghosh marshalled out, suspended after assembly ruckus
content: Trinamool Congress MLA Kunal Ghosh faced suspension from the West Bengal Assembly. He disrupted proceedings by interrupting a fellow legislator during a budget debate. Marshals forcibly removed Ghosh after he refused to return to his seat. This incident highlighted internal rifts within the Trinamool Congress legislature party. Assembly proceedings resumed only after the legislator was escorted from the House.

title: Firouzja wins Chennai Grand Masters as Gukesh suffers shock last-place finish
content: French Grandmaster Alireza Firouzja secured the Chennai Grand Masters title. He held Indian GM Arjun Erigaisi to a draw in the final round. World champion D Gukesh endured a disappointing campaign, finishing last in the tournament. Firouzja finished with 4.5 points after two victories and five draws. Andreikin ended the event as the only other unbeaten player.

title: How the world's happiest nation plans to stop youth exodus
content: Bhutan, known for its unique happiness philosophy, is experiencing a troubling trend of youth emigration. In response, the government is introducing Gelephu Mindfulness City, which aims to seamlessly integrate economic advancement with cultural preservation and ecological sustainability. Supported by India through infrastructure development and rail connectivity, this initiative hopes to inspire young Bhutanese to choose to stay and thrive in their country.

title: 'US will destroy one bridge or power plant': Trump warns Iran over Hormuz attacks
content: United States forces will destroy Iranian infrastructure if Tehran fires at ships. This threat comes after recent American airstrikes and Iranian missile attacks on Jordan. Diplomatic efforts show little progress as officials remain firm on their positions. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz significantly impacts global energy markets and economies. Houthi rebels in Yemen also threaten shipping, putting another trade chokepoint at risk.

title: Jaffer says Rahul 'is at par' with Kohli, reveals one quality that separates them
content: Wasim Jaffer believes KL Rahul possesses talent equal to Virat Kohli. He noted Rahul's technical skill and composure in all formats. Jaffer feels Rahul has underachieved despite his considerable potential. Kohli's elite mentality and consistency create a significant difference between the two players. This mental edge has allowed Kohli to achieve unprecedented success in cricket.

title: Universal provident fund scheme: How PF for gig workers, self-employed could work
content: The proposed framework aims to allow unorganised sector workers and self-employed to set aside a portion of their income for deposits under a universal provident fund scheme which will earn regular interest on par with the existing EPFO scheme.

title: 'Lots of positives': Iyer on 'rough' England tour, urges fearless approach in Zimbabwe
content: India captain Shreyas Iyer views the recent England tour as a foundation for future success.  He believes the challenging phase has brought the squad closer together.  Iyer urged his young team to embrace an attacking mindset and not fear failure.  The team faces Zimbabwe in a three-match T20I series starting Thursday.  He also warned against underestimating the experienced Zimbabwe squad.

title: Politics of silence: Is Sharad Pawar keeping options open on Delimitation Bill?
content: The silence of Sharad Pawar's NCP(SP) regarding the Delimitation Bill is stirring political rumors. While Sharad Pawar joined the opposition for demonstrations on Tuesday, he met Prime Minister Narendra Modi the next day. Supriya Sule, his daughter, expressed conditional support for Bill if it proposed seat increases across states evenly. This reflects Pawar's history of maintaining strategic ambiguity, thereby ensuring his party remains adaptable within shifting political currents.

title: Delhi HC dismisses PIL over Sonam Wangchuk’s removal from Jantar Mantar
content: “Since an FIR is sought to be lodged in relation to a reportedly solitary incident, it is always open for the petitioner to take recourse to 173 of BNSS,” says court

title: NEET’s promise has failed; it’s time to trust States, writes M.K. Stalin
content: The student protests must serve as a wake-up call — not merely to improve exam security but to fundamentally rethink the entire framework of high-stakes national entrance exams

title: Maharashtra begins rollout of Farmer Loan Waiver Scheme; first 500 beneficiaries announced
content: Lists will be published at the village level in the Gram Panchayat, various executive cooperative society offices and the respective bank branches, says CMO

title: ‘Over 5,200 applications’: Ram Temple trust defers CEO selection by a month
content: In second meeting amid donation theft row, trust announces reconstituted committee of seers to oversee religious affairs of temple; secretary to be appointed on interim basis until CEO is picked

title: BJP, Oppn. take divergent view of police action on protesters
content: Monday’s march was ‘not peaceful’ and its organisers must be held accountable, says Delhi Minister; BJP MP says Centre acted with ‘sensitivity’; AAP highlights instances of women being ‘assaulted’; cadre standing with Rahul: Delhi Cong. chief

title: Diesel generator at textile showroom in Katpadi catches fire
content: The police said that it was around 2.30 p.m. when a few workers in the showroom noticed thick smoke coming from the diesel generator

title: Two-day Thiruvasagam conference to be held in Tiruvannamalai
content: As part of the event, around one lakh participants worldwide will participate, mainly virtually, in writing 10 compositions from Thiruvasagam

title: Meet us here, CJP tells govt. as crowd swells at Jantar Mantar
content: If Ministers want to talk, they should come to people, says CJP; group appeals to Wangchuk to end fast; people turn up in thousands at Jantar Mantar; some of the protesters injured in police action back at venue

title: Doctors in Kerala’s Nilambur save tribal woman, baby after rare uterine rupture
content: The 25-year-old woman from the Cheerakkuzhi tribal hamlet near Chugathara was brought to the Government District Hospital Nilambur hospital with a rare uterine rupture that left the baby lying inside her abdominal cavity

title: City hospital launches integrated robotic surgery programme
content: The initiative brings robotic-assisted surgery across multiple specialties under a common clinical framework

title: Doctor in Kerala’s Kannur booked over alleged medical negligence, hospital denies charges
content: The complainant alleges she was discharged after an endoscopic procedure despite a fish bone remaining lodged in her throat

title: SHITIJ 2.0: nationwide start-up incubation programme inaugurated at ICAR-CPCRI
content: The second edition of SHITIJ is being implemented through a nationwide network of seven hub institutes and their associated spoke institutes

title: Activists, celebrities join Kochi protests in solidarity with Delhi students
content: Writers, filmmakers, actors and activists back agitation over the alleged NEET paper leak and police action in Delhi; solidarity protests held across Kochi, with marches, public meetings and a candlelight vigil drawing students and youngsters; speakers criticise alleged police excesses and call for wider public support for the student movement

title: Kerala UDF constituent IUML trains guns on Revenue Minister A.P. Anil Kumar
content: IUML leaders from the Wandoor constituency accuse Mr. Anil Kumar of sidelining the party after assuming office as Revenue Minister

title: Why so selective on paper leaks, Nadda asks Rahul
content: J.P. Nadda said Rahul Gandhi did not mention incidents of paper leaks and related irregularities reported under governments led by Opposition parties, including the Congress

title: Piracy fails to dampen advance bookings for Chief Minister Vijay’s Jana Nayagan
content: According to data shared by trade tracker Sacnilk, Jana Nayagan has sold 7.37 lakh tickets nationwide, and grossed ₹16.78 crore in advance bookings across 9,566 shows

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Heavy security deployed at Jantar Mantar, as crowd of protesters swells
content: Rahul Gandhi using students' issues for political mileage, says J.P. Nadda; Wangchuk seeks 'unequivocal assurance' that no young protester will face punitive, retaliatory legal action

title: Did not refuse to meet KSU leaders, says Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan
content: Chief Minister’s comments came in response to reports that he had refused to meet KSU president Aloshious Xavier amid controversies over the appointment of an individual with alleged past links to the Students Federation of India  as government pleader

title: SFI demands that UDF government rid the Kerala Governor of Chancellor power
content: Other demands include free bus travel for students, permanent Vice-Chancellors, free education till degree level, political freedom on campuses, permanent Principals in government colleges, regulating fees in private and self-financing institutions, and a unified examination system

title: Madras High Court dismisses Senthilbalaji’s plea to prevent police from ‘harassing’ him and his brother
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan says, no such direction can be issued when the brothers had obtained anticipatory bail in pending cases and it is premature to issue such a direction with respect to future cases

title: Government allays fears over FCRA Bill, clarifies on designated authority
content: PIB says the Bill won’t alter religious character of places of worship attached by designated authority

title: Body of Uttar Pradesh native fished out of Vilangad river
content: The body of Nigam Kumar was recovered from Vilangad river after a four-day search with the support of various rescue teams

title: Lawyers back protesters, urge PM to order probe on police crackdown
content: Supreme Court Bar Association adopted a resolution seeking an immediate, impartial and time-bound inquiry into the police action, stating that several students and members of the legal fraternity were injured

title: Sikkim NHPC tunnel disaster: Search for Malayali geologist continues
content: The tunnel under construction collapsed following an explosion in the ‘Head Race Tunnel’ and a geologist from Kottayam is feared trapped inside the tunnel after a landslide blocked its entrance

title: Student bodies to hold ‘Chalo Lok Bhavan’ protest on July 23
content: They seek Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak and call for support to the July 24 educational institutions bandh in Andhra Pradesh

title: Chipko movement was a challenge to hierarchy of development: Murali Sivaramakrishnan
content: Discussion held on the book 50 Years of Chipko – the Movement and its Afterlives  that has been edited by him

title: NEET: Congress, BJP workers detained during rival protests in Bengaluru
content: R. Ashok has alleged attack by Congress cadre and submitted a petition to the Bengaluru City Police Commissioner

title: 19-year-old may lose vision in right eye due to pellet injury, says family
content: Sahil Lochab underwent a surgery to remove pellets; doctors have informed that there is only a “1% chance” of vision returning in the injured eye as the pupil was damaged, his family said

title: Centre steps up outreach to Wangchuk, agitators; activist puts a rider to call off protest
content: Will call off hunger strike if govt. promises no action against students, says Wangchuk; “we are in talks with CJP and will continue to engage,” says Nadda, main interlocutor

title: PM should apologise to students, Education Minister must resign: Rahul Gandhi
content: The Congress leader says there have been 156 paper leaks and ‘zero convictions’, the Opposition is ‘100% in agreement’ with the protesters’ demands, describing them as ‘legitimate’ and ‘non-negotiable’

title: ACB books energy utility divisional engineer in disproportionate assets case, seizes properties worth ₹27.65 crore
content: Ramana had earlier been trapped by ACB in December 2019 while serving as Divisional Engineer (Technical) in the Cyber City Circle of the Hyderabad electricity department.

title: Eastern Assam floods worst in 60 years, says State Minister
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says the damage is far greater than anticipated and more bodies are being recovered from the affected districts; more rains forecast over next four days

title: Opposition, government spar over NEET debate in Rajya Sabha; House adjourned repeatedly
content: The government has been saying since the beginning of the session... all important issues, including NEET paper leak, will be discussed, said Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiran Rijiju

title: 50 OTT platforms disabled for displaying obscene content, other violations: Govt.
content: IT Rules require social media platforms to remove unlawful content within three hours of the receipt of a court order or reasoned intimation by the government or its agency

title: Anna writes to PM: Stop muzzling people, hold talks to resolve their issues
content: To maintain people’s trust is the greatest responsibility of an elected government, the activist said

title: Revocation of bail warranted only if continuing liberty is ‘so egregious’ that it undermines justice: Supreme Court
content: Apex court refuses to cancel bail granted by High Court to Chaitanya Baghel, son of former Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Bhupesh Baghel, in cases arising out of an alleged liquor scam

title: Haryana Congress seeks Murmu’s intervention on ‘paper leaks’
content: Haryana Congress In-charge Sanjay Dutt, along with other leaders, submits a memorandum to the President through Deputy Commissioner of Kurukshetra district

title: ‘Toxic gas’ kills two ‘illegal miners’ in abandoned Assam coal field
content: A rights body claims the duo died after inhaling toxic gas in an illegal mine, while the Tinsukia district police say the cause of death is under investigation

title: Rahul Gandhi reassures allies, says Opposition will maintain close coordination on protest strategies
content: At INDIA bloc meet, Congress leader tries to smooth ruffled egos; says allies were not deliberately kept out of the loop on the protest at the PM’s residence; insists decision was sudden, could not be communicated in time

title: MoJS asks State to return ₹36.34 cr. released under PMKSY with 7% interest
content: Audit finds that the amount lies with State govt. as  it is unused for 11 projects for which it was released or for 9 projects for which the State wanted adjustment

title: Pellet guns, shock batons used against protesters in Delhi; police deny
content: Shaikh Irshad Mansoori suffered pellet injuries, the first known case of security forces using pellet guns in Delhi; police deny use, say public must “verify facts”

title: Threat to CM Vijay: Vilathikulam DMK MLA’s bail plea dismissed
content: The prosecution said the bail petition should be rejected as the accused had issued serious threat to the Chief Minister

title: What caused the NHPC tunnel disaster in Sikkim’s Namchi? | Explained
content: Methane trapped inside rock is suspected to have triggered the fatal blast. But how does this happen?

title: Terrorism Trial Opens In Stabbing Of Author Salman Rushdie
content: The man convicted of trying to kill Salman Rushdie spent years researching the writer's whereabouts before stabbing him on a lecture stage, federal prosecutors said Wednesday at the opening of his federal terrorism trial.

title: "Eye For An Eye": Iran Warns Of Forceful Response To US Strike
content: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Wednesday that Iran would respond in kind to any attack on its infrastructure, after Washington threatened to bomb a bridge or power plant for every ship targeted in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: "Want Better Future": 13-Year-Old On Why She Joined CJP's Protest
content: Many young men and women said the protesters are at the protest site for all students and children, who are the future of the nation.

title: "98% Of Policemen Suffered Only Minor Injuries": CJP Spokespersons To NDTV
content: Spokespersons Saurav Das and Ashutosh Ranka said they were not worried about other parties overshadowing their protest.

title: Iran Strikes On CIA Facilities Prompt Questions About Possible Russian Role
content: Iranian drone attacks on CIA facilities in the Gulf have prompted US intelligence analysts to investigate whether Russia assisted by providing targeting information or advanced drone technology, said four people familiar with US intelligence.

title: Exclusive | "Students Betrayed By System": Shashi Tharoor On Paper Leak Protests
content: On Rahul Gandhi's detention outside the PM's residence, Shashi Tharoor said that was not how a mature democracy should behave.

title: Iran-US War News Live Updates: Trump Attends Transfer Of Fallen US Service Members
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

title: Trump's Saudi Nuclear Pact Opens Door To Uranium Enrichment. What's At Stake
content: The agreement leaves out two safeguards Washington has traditionally insisted on. First, a ban on uranium enrichment and spent fuel reprocessing, along with an "additional protocol" that provides tougher international inspections.

title: China "Ready" To Handle Sensitive Issues With India, Says Foreign Minister
content: Both Wang and Jaishankar are in Manila to attend meetings related to the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN).

title: Two Life Convicts, Serving Time For Murder Marry Inside Jodhpur Jail
content: With jail officials, police personnel and prison staff serving as witnesses, the couple completed the customary Hindu wedding rituals.

title: 13 Bodies Recovered From Collapsed Sikkim Tunnel, Rescue Ops Continue
content: The body of one more worker was recovered from the NHPC Teesta Stage VI Hydropower Project tunnel on Wednesday during search operations, taking the number of bodies retrieved so far to 13, officials said.

title: Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Says Uniform Code Bill Likely In Winter Session
content: The chief minister stated that, in addition to the "Mahatari Vandan Yojana," the state government is taking several major steps to empower women economically.

title: Over 2,000 Detained Across J&K After Cop Killed In Anantnag Terrorist Attack
content: Head Constable Aashiq Hussain Qureshi, 35, was killed when terrorists opened fire at a police party deployed at a busy market area in Anantnag

title: 1989-Batch IAS Officer Anurag Jain Named NITI Aayog's New CEO
content: Jain is a 1989-batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer of the Madhya Pradesh cadre and has held several key positions in the Centre and the state government during his administrative career.

title: Elon Musk Promises 'True To Homer' AI Version Of Odyssey After Nolan's Release
content: The billionaire said the AI-generated film would be released before the end of the year and would stay faithful to Homer's original work.

title: Sharad Pawar, Supriya Sule Meet PM Modi, Discuss NEET Paper Leak
content: After the meeting with PM Modi, Pawar said he and Sule discussed the students' demands regarding the NEET paper "leak", farmers' problems, water management and issues concerning the education sector with the prime minister.

title: 5-Year-Old Girl Killed After Tree Collapses On School Bus In Karnataka
content: The accident occurred near Kalleti in Bantwal taluk of Dakshina Kannada district when the bus was dropping the pre-primary students home after school.

title: How A Paper Leak Protest In 1997 Made Dharmendra Pradhan A Student Leader
content: Dharmendra Pradhan hit the streets of Bhubaneswar as a student leader associated with the Akhil Bharatiya Vidyarthi Parishad (ABVP) to protest against the Janaki Patnaik's government.

title: Delhi Teacher Dies Of Heart Attack, Family Alleges SIR Related Stress
content: Suman Lata, a nursery teacher posted at an MCD primary school in Kapashera, died of heart attack Wednesday morning. She is survived by her husband, a son and a daughter.

title: Adani Defence To Develop Next-Gen Airborne Surveillance System For Air Force
content: Under the agreement, Adani Defence & Aerospace and DRDO will jointly develop, manufacture and integrate the airborne mission systems on six modified Airbus A321 aircraft for the IAF.

title: Soldier slips to death in J&K’s Rajouri
content: the deceased, Rifleman Sukhpreet Singh, was a native of Punjab . he was on duty at Mahadev forward post when he suddenly lost his balance and fell from a height . the body was shifted to a hospital for post-mortem .

title: Sharad Pawar meets PM Modi, timing fuels buzz of delimit bill talk
content: the meeting focused on Maharashtra's agrarian crisis . pawar raised demands including a legal guarantee for minimum support price . supriya Sule invited Modi to her daughter's wedding reception in Delhi .
